summary: Every branch has a birth and a death — a mandatory entry gate and exit gate — and worktree isolation carries the same lifecycle discipline: one task, one unique fresh branch, cleaned up the moment its work ships.

rules.rule: Before creating any branch, pass the entry gate: on the default branch, clean working tree, local branch count under threshold, zero stashes, no stuck rebase/merge — if any check fails, fix it first, do not create the branch.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Edit files on the default branch and then stash/branch — create the branch before editing any file; branch first, edit second.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Immediately after merge, run the exit gate: remote branch deleted, local branch deleted, back on the default branch, merged changes pulled, working tree clean, stash list empty — emitting an exit state; if any step fails, fix it before anything else.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Reuse an existing branch without explicit owner confirmation, reuse a stale leftover worktree directory (remove and recreate fresh), or key a worktree on a numeric/truncated prefix — the worktree id is the full work-item slug, and a collision with a different branch/work-item means ABORT.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Remove the worktree immediately after merge, use squash-aware merged-branch detection (never rely on 'git branch --merged' alone), and periodically audit the physical worktree-root directory — not only the registry — for abandoned and orphaned dirs.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Treat the default (main) worktree as read-only — only git sync and the create-only comms lane are permitted, all other work happens in a feature branch/worktree, and any emergency override documents its reason in the commit.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
